---
title: "Core Python"
description: "Learn the small building blocks that almost every Python program uses: values, names, decisions, and repetition."
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [core-python, beginner]
order: 80
colab_notebook: "notebooks/lessons/core-python/index.ipynb"
---
{{< include ../_includes/colab-link.qmd >}}

<!-- fcpython-chapter-lab: start -->
## How to use this chapter hands-on

This chapter is about **Core Python**. Do not read it like a reference manual.
Use it as a sequence of short labs. For each lesson, open the Colab notebook
from the button above, run Example 1, write a prediction, and then
change one small part of the code.

By the end of the chapter, you should have one small artifact: a notebook, a
script, a trace table, or a project note. The artifact should show one working
example, one controlled variation, and one error or surprising result that you
investigated calmly.

If time is short, study one lesson deeply instead of skimming all of them.
A deeply understood example is one you can run again, explain in plain
language, modify safely, and debug when it breaks.
<!-- fcpython-chapter-lab: end -->


## Why this chapter matters

Learn the small building blocks that almost every Python program uses: values, names, decisions, and repetition.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.callout-note title="Guiding questions"}
- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Values, Variables, and Types](values-variables-types.qmd) — Understand values, expressions, assignment, names, and type conversion together.
- [Strings, Input, and Output](strings-input-output.qmd) — Work with text, formatted messages, and simple user interaction.
- [Booleans and Conditionals](booleans-and-conditionals.qmd) — Ask yes/no questions and choose different paths with if, elif, and else.
- [Loops and Tracing](loops-and-tracing.qmd) — Repeat work with for and while loops, control loop flow, and trace changing values.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "core-python-overview",
  "title": "Core Python overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Core Python chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Learn the small building blocks that almost every Python program uses: values, names, decisions, and repetition.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Learn the small building blocks that almost every Python program uses: values, names, decisions, and repetition."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
